info.title: Root
info.description: Root is an end-to-end digital insurance platform that enables you to launch new products and digital engagement channels fast.

post.description: **Note:** Additional custom fields may be required depending on how the [application hook](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/application-hook) is configured for the product module.

quote_package_id.description: Must be a UUID. Unique identifier of quote package retrieved in [quote generation](ref:getting-a-quote-2).

policyholder_id.description: Must be a UUID. Unique identifier of the [policyholder](ref:policyholder) that is applying.

billing_day.description: The day of month on which the policy to write will be billed on. Should be between 1 and 31. If it falls on a day that does not exist in the month (for example, 31 in February) the policy to be written will be billed on the last day of that month. Setting this value to 31 will ensure that the policy to be written will be billed on the last day of every month.

monthly_premium.description: `null` is allowed. The monthly premium amount in cents. Minimum value is 0.

type.description: The type of the application. One of `requote` or `new_policy`.

user_group_id.description: `null` is allowed. Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the user group to assign this application to.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: A space seperated list of matching words to search for. e.g. "richard@aviato.com"

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: A comma seperated list of statuses. eg. "pending,issued"

parameters.name: created_by
parameters.description: A valid UUID of the user that created the application.

parameters.name: user_group_id
parameters.description: A valid UUID of the user group that created the application.

parameters.name: created_by_type
parameters.description: A semi-colon seperated list of the requestee types. e.g. api_key, user, legacy_api_key, legacy_user_api_key, system, product_module or embed_jwt

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: An optional semicolon-separated list of underlying objects to include, e.g. `?include=policy;policyholder`. Valid options are 'policy', 'policyholder' and 'quote_package'

parameters.name: product_module_id
parameters.description: A valid UUID of the product module to filter applications by.

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the [application](ref:application).

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: An optional semicolon-separated list of underlying objects to include, e.g. `?include=policy;policyholder`. Valid options are 'policy', 'policyholder' and 'quote_package'

post.description: Creates a new call record with an associated recording. The recording must be provided as a base64-encoded string.

claim_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the [claim](ref:claim) for which the call is being made.

policyholder_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the [policyholder](ref:policyholder) for whom the call is being made.

from.description: The phone number the call was made from.

to.description: The phone number the call was made to.

topic.description: The topic category of the call.

call_duration.description: The duration of the call in seconds.

recording_file_type.description: The MIME type of the recording. Must be `audio/mpeg`.

recording_base64_string.description: The base64-encoded string of the call recording.

parameters.name: call_id
parameters.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the [call](ref:call).

get.description: Returns a list of events associated with a specific call, sorted by creation time in descending order (most recent first). Events include call status changes, recordings added, and other call-related activities.

parameters.name: call_id
parameters.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the [call](ref:call).

items.description: An event object containing details about an action that occurred on the call.

type.description: The type of call event. Possible values include: `inbound_call_started`, `outbound_call_started`, `option_entered`, `recording_added`, `call_initiated`, `call_ringing`, `call_answered`, `call_completed`, `call_not_answered`, `called_busy`, `call_flushed`, `call_created`, `call_updated`, `connect_call_initiated`, `connect_call_ringing`, `connect_call_answered`, `connect_call_completed`, `connect_call_not_answered`, `connect_called_busy`.

call_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the call.

created_at.description: The timestamp at which the event occurred.

requested_by.description: An object indicating the user or API key that triggered the event.

type.description: The type of requester (e.g., `user`, `api_key`).

id.description: The unique identifier of the requester.

owner_id.description: The owner ID associated with the requester.

to_number.description: The destination phone number. Only present for `connect_call_initiated` events.

from.description: The originating phone number. Present for `inbound_call_started` and `outbound_call_started` events.

to.description: The destination phone number. Present for `outbound_call_started` events.

option.description: The option entered by the caller. Only present for `option_entered` events.

recording_url.description: The URL of the call recording. Only present for `recording_added` events.

get.description: Returns a signed URL for playing back the recording of a specific call. If the call has an associated attachment, a signed download URL is returned. Otherwise, the legacy recording URL is returned if available. Returns a 404 error if no recording exists for the call.

parameters.name: call_id
parameters.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the [call](ref:call).

url.description: The URL to play back the call recording. This may be a signed download URL (for newer recordings stored as attachments) or a legacy recording URL.

file_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the attachment file. Only present if the recording is stored as an attachment.

file_type.description: The MIME type of the recording file (e.g., `audio/mpeg`). Only present if the recording is stored as an attachment.

file_name.description: The filename of the recording. Only present if the recording is stored as an attachment.

file_url.description: The permanent download URL for the file. Only present if the recording is stored as an attachment.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the attachment was created. Only present if the recording is stored as an attachment.

404.description: Not Found - No recording exists for this call

example.message: No recording url or attachment found for call with id d3d13c48-4dc3-4816-8d01-de3215878225

post.description: Creates a new claim. The claim can optionally be linked to a policy, member, or covered item. A claimant can be specified with contact details.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of related objects to include in the response. Valid values: `policy`, `policyholder`, `user_group`.

policy_id.description: Must be a UUID. The identifier of the [policy](ref:policy) on which the claim is being made.

member_id.description: Must be a UUID. The identifier of the member for this claim. Only used for group scheme policies.

covered_item_id.description: Must be a UUID. The identifier of the covered item that this claim is against. Required if the policy supports covered items.

assigned_to_user_id.description: Must be a UUID. The identifier of the user to assign this claim to.

incident_type.description: A description of the incident type.

incident_cause.description: A description of the cause of the incident.

incident_date.description: The date on which the incident occurred.

app_data.description: An object containing additional custom data for the claim.

claimant.description: Object containing claimant's details. At least one of `email` or `cellphone` must be provided.

first_name.description: The first name of the claimant.

last_name.description: The last name of the claimant.

email.description: The claimant's email address. Required if `cellphone` is not provided.

cellphone.description: The claimant's cellphone number. Required if `email` is not provided.

id_number.description: The ID number of the claimant.

relationship.description: The relationship of the claimant to the policyholder.

requested_amount.description: The requested payout amount (in cents) for the claim.

post.description: This endpoint is deprecated in favour of [create payment](ref:createpolicypayment). This endpoint validates the input and queues payments to be processed asynchronously. A response of `200 OK` is sent if the payments are valid and have been successfully queued. This endpoint allows for up to 5000 payments to be created at a time. To use this endpoint you'll need the `Payments Create` permission.

This endpoint validates the input and queues [payment updates](ref:payment-update) to be processed asynchronously, which will result in a `200 OK` successful response. **Note:** Despite a successful response, queued updates can nevertheless fail. To receive the update failure / success status, subscribe to the `payment_updates_succeeded` and `payment_updates_failed` [webhooks](ref:create-webhook).

post.description: Once a policy has been issued, it is important that a receipt is created which verifies that the new policyholder has received their actual policy. This receipt can either be in the form of a tracking pixel in an email which contains the policy document, or it could be a custom method (subject to approval). To add a tracking pixel to a policy email, you create an `` tag which looks like the following: ```html ``` Important to note is that the tag includes the policy version number and the policy unique identifier in the query string, while the image name that of your `organisation_id`. Alternatively if you wish to use a custom receipt method, you can `POST` to the receipt endpoint. This endpoint accepts any valid JSON object, but MUST include the version number in the body. The body value should be any data which could be reasonably said to be evidence of the policyholder receiving the policy document.

application.description:
An application represents a "draft" policy, with all information captured and validated. Applications persist on Root (they do not expire), and can be reviewed and managed on the Root management dashboard.

See the [issing policies](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/policy-issuing) guide and the [application hook](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/application-hook) guide for more details.

If it falls on a day that does not exist in the month (for example, 31 in February) the policy to be written will be billed on the last day of that month. Setting this value to 31 will ensure that the policy to be written will be billed on the last day of every month.

policy.description:
Policies are issued from applications, and represent binding insurance agreements. Parameters stored on the policy can be referenced in custom notifications, policy documents, data exports and external integrations.

See the [issuing policies](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/policy-issuing) guide and the [policy issue hook](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/policy-issue-hook) guide for more details.

policy_id.description: Must be a UUID. Object ID of the policy.

policy_number.description: A shorter, human-readable policy identifier. This typically used in policy documents and customer notifications for ease of reference.

policyholder_id.description: Must be a UUID. The system identifier of the policy's policyholder.

sum_assured.description: The amount, in cents, of the maximum value insured.

monthly_premium.description: The total monthly premium, in cents, that will be billed to the policyholder.

billing_amount.description: The amount, in cents, that will be billed on the next billing run.

balance.description: The amount, in cents, that is owed on the policy. If the policyholder makes an adhoc payment to the policy, the amount is credited to this balance. If the policyholder defaults on a payment, it is subtracted from the balance. If the balance is found negative during the next billing run, the amount is added to the monthly premium, else the monthly premium is subtracted from the balance and the remainder is charged.

billing_day.description: `null` is allowed. The day of month on which the policy is billed. Should be between 1 and 31, or `null`. If it falls on a day that does not exist in the month (for example, 31 in February) the policy will be billed on the last day of the month. Setting this value to 31 will ensure that the policy is billed on the last day of every month.

billing_frequency.description: The frequency at which the policy is billed. One of [`monthly`, `yearly`, `once_off`]. See the [billing settings](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/billing-settings#billing-frequency) guide for more details on the billing frequency.

next_billing_date.description: The next date the policy is due to be billed.

complaint_ids.description: An array of complaint ids of the complaints linked to the policy.

start_date.description: Once the policy reaches its start date, cover commences and regular collection attempts can run against the policy (provided it has an `active` status). See the [Managing policies](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/policy-administration#policy-lifecycle) guide for more details on the policy lifecycle.

end_date.description: When the policy reaches its end date, its status will change to `expired`. Cover and collections will cease. Policies with no end date do not expire.

registration_number.description: (Company policyholders only) The company's registration number.

date_of_establishment.description: (Company policyholders only) The company's date of establishment in the format YYYYMMDD.

company_website_url.description: (Company policyholders only) The company's website URL.

contact_position.description: (Company policyholders only) The position or role of the primary contact at the company.

subsidiary_companies.description: (Company policyholders only) An array of subsidiary company names.

email.description: `null` is allowed. The policyholder or primary contact's contact email address.

policy_ids.description: An array of ids of the policies linked to this policyholder.

policies.description: An array of full [policy objects](#policy) owned by the policyholder. This is currently only included when [fetching all policyholders](#list-all-policyholders) or [fetching an individual policyholder](#retrieve-a-policyholder) with `?include=policies`.

created_at.description: The time at which the policyholder was created.

app_data.description: `null` is allowed. An object containing additional custom data for the policy holder.

created_by.description: `null` is allowed. An object indicating the user or API key that created the policyholder. See [Authentication](#client-apps).

updated_at.description: The time at which the policyholder was last updated.

address.description: `null` is allowed. The policyholder's physical address. See below for more details.

line_1.description: First line of the policyholder's address.

line_2.description: `null` is allowed. Optional second line of the policyholder's address.

country.description: The policyholder's country code, in the [ISO Alpha-2](http://www.nationsonline.org/oneworld/country_code_list.htm) format.

area_code.description: The policyholder's area code must be 4 characters long for South African addresses.

quote-package.description:
Quote packages are designed to be low-fidelity and fast to generate, and are used to generate and display new quotes rapidly on a customer-facing front-end. They expire after one week.

See the [issing policies](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/policy-issuing) guide and the [quote hook](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/quote-hook) guide for more details.

quote_package_id.description: Must be a UUID. Unique identifier of the quote package.

sum_assured.description: The maximum insured value in cents.

base_premium.description: The combined risk and platform fee in cents. `monthly_premium` should be calculated from this.

suggested_premium.description: The suggested `monthly_premium` amount in cents. This is the total premium for display to the customer.

module.description: Custom, product-specific information saved to the quote for later reference.  These parameters are set in the [quote hook](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/quote-hook) in the product module code.

restricted_data.description: Optional restricted product-specific information saved to the quote. This field is only returned when the requestee has access to restricted quote module data.

input_data.description: Quote input parameters as received over the [create a quote](https://docs.rootplatform.com/reference/getting-a-quote-2) endpoint.

created_at.description: The date/time at which the quote package was created.

currency.description: The currency used for the quote package, in [ISO 4217] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.

billing_frequency.description: The frequency at which the policy will be billed. See the [billing settings](https://docs.rootplatform.com/docs/billing-settings#billing-frequency) guide for more details on the billing frequency.

product_module_definition_id.description: Must be a UUID. Unique identifier of the product module definition associated with this quote package. If a policy is issued from this quote, this product module definition will be associated with the policy.

type.description: The type of adapter. In this case, `s3`.

bucket_name.description: The name of the S3 bucket.

bucket_path.description: The path of the S3 bucket to store the resulting export to, with a trailing slash. This is used to generate a bucket key e.g. `folder/subfolder/`.

secret_access_key.description: An AWS secret access key with permission to read from and write to the bucket.

access_key_id.description: An AWS access key ID with permission to read from and write to the bucket.

scheduled_data_export_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the scheduled data export.

environment.description: The dataset used by the scheduled data export. Either `sandbox` or `production`.

created_at.description: The time at which the scheduled data export was created.

created_by.description: An object indicating the user or API key that created the scheduled data export. See [Authentication](#client-apps).

status.description: The current status of the scheduled data export. One of `active`, `paused`, or `archived`.

name.description: The name of the scheduled data export.

frequency.description: The frequency at which the scheduled data export runs. See below for more details.

adapter.description: The adapter used to send the export once it's compiled. See below for more details.

export_type.description: The type of scheduled data export. Only `policy_export` is currently supported.

data_range.description: The range used to capture data for the export. One of `today`, `week_to_date`, `month_to_date`, `since_last_run`, or `full`.

latest_run.description: The latest run for the scheduled data export, if one exists. See below for more details.

type.description: The type of adapter. In this case, `sftp`.

host.description: The SFTP IP address or hostname.

port.description: The port number to connect to on the host. Defaults to `22` if not provided.

path.description: The path on the server to store the resulting export in.

username.description: The username to use when authenticating on the SFTP server. Defaults to anonymous authentication if not provided.

password.description: The password to use when authenticating on the SFTP server. Defaults to anonymous authentication if not provided.

webhook_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook.

organization_id.description: The unique identifier of the organization the webhook belongs to.

environment.description: The environment associated with the webhook.

description.description: An optional description for the webhook.

secret.description: The auto-generated webhook secret, used to verify the payload (see the [introduction](ref:webhooks) for more details).

verification_token.description: An optional token to set on the webhook, used as an additional verification step for the payload (see the [introduction](ref:webhooks) for more details).

url.description: The URL that the webhook will `POST` payloads to.

priority.description: The priority of the webhook. Attempts for a webhook with lower value will be prioritized over a higher value.

enabled.description: Whether the webhook is enabled or not. If `false`, all queue events are marked as `failed` without being attempted.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the webhook was created.

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the webhook was last updated.

user_group_id.description: The unique identifier of the user group associated with the webhook. When set, only events from entities belonging to this user group will trigger the webhook.

example.name: Example webhook
example.description: A webhook for receiving various events

webhook_queue_event_id.description: The identifier of the webhook queue event.

environment.description: The environment in which the queue event occurred.

webhook_id.description: The identifier of the webhook associated with this queue event.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the webhook queue event was created.

status.description: The current status of the webhook queue event.

subscription.description: The subscription event type for this webhook queue event.

url.description: The URL to which the webhook queue event will be sent via `POST`.

attempts_count.description: The number of attempts made to process this webhook queue event.

body.description: The payload body of the webhook queue event.

webhook_id.description: The unique ID of the webhook.

event.description: The event data - see [webhook subscriptions](ref:webhooks#available-subscriptions) for available event types.

verification_token.description: An optional verification token as provided during creating a webhook.

environment.description: The dataset used by the webhook. Either sandbox or production.

last_attempt_at.description: The timestamp of the last attempt to process this webhook queue event.

last_response_code.description: The HTTP response code from the last attempt to process this webhook queue event.

last_response_time_millis.description: The response time of the last attempt to send the webhook queue event to the URL in milliseconds.

webhook-subscription-event.description: The key identifying the subscription event.

webhook-subscription-events.description: An array of events that this webhook should subscribe to. If you want to  subscribe to an event not listed here, please submit an idea on  our [product roadmap portal](https://portal.productboard.com/root-platform/) describing your use case.

time_of_day.description: The time of day to run the export, in `HH:mm` format.

day_of_week.description: The day of week to run the export. One of `monday`, `tuesday`, `wednesday`, `thursday`, `friday`, `saturday`, or `sunday`.

cellphone.description: `null` and `""` allowed. Object containing the cellphone number in national format, and the corresponding alpha 2 country code.

country.description: The [ISO Alpha-2](http://www.nationsonline.org/oneworld/country_code_list.htm) country code of the country of the cellphone number.

cellphone-string.description: `null` and `""` allowed. String containing the cellphone number in international format.

beneficiaries-request.description: `null` is allowed. An array of beneficiaries. If null, the default beneficiary may be the policyholder if enabled in the product module settings. Some modules may require adding beneficiaries to a policy for claims to be processed.

type.description: The type of beneficiary to be created.

id.description: (Individual beneficiaries only) Object containing beneficiary's identification number, type and country.

type.description: The beneficiary's identification type.

number.description: Depending on the type selected, either an ID number, passport number or custom identifier.

date_of_birth.description: `null` is allowed. An optional string containing the beneficiary's date of birth.

percentage.description: An integer representing the percentage of a claim payout that the beneficiary should receive. Note that the percentages across all beneficiaries should add up to 100%.

company_name.description: The name of the company that the beneficiary represents. Required when `type = company`.

registration_number.description: The company's registration number. Required when `type = company`.

policyholder_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of an existing policyholder to link as a beneficiary.

line_1.description: First line of the beneficiary's address.

line_2.description: `null` is allowed. Optional second line of the beneficiary's address.

country.description: The beneficiary's country code, in the [ISO Alpha-2](http://www.nationsonline.org/oneworld/country_code_list.htm) format.

beneficiaries.description: `null` is allowed. An array of beneficiaries. If null, the default beneficiary may be the policyholder if enabled in the product module settings. Some product modules may require adding beneficiaries to a policy for claims to be processed.

beneficiary_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the beneficiary.

policyholder_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the policyholder. Only relevant if `relationship` is `policyholder`.

type.description: The type of beneficiary.

id.description: (Individual beneficiaries only) The beneficiary's identification number, type and country.

type.description: The beneficiary's identification type.

number.description: Depending on the type selected, either an ID number, passport number or custom identifier.

date_of_birth.description: `null` is allowed. The beneficiary's date of birth.

percentage.description: An integer representing the percentage of a claim payout that the beneficiary should receive. Note that the percentages across all beneficiaries should add up to 100%.

company_name.description: The name of the company that the beneficiary represents. Required when `type = company`.

registration_number.description: The company's registration number. Required when `type = company`.

type.description: The policyholder type.

id.description: (Individual policyholders only) Object containing policyholder's identification information.

type.description: The id type of the policyholder.

number.description: Depending on the `type` selected, either an ID number, passport number, email address, cellphone number or custom identifier.

expiration_date.description: (Individual policyholders only) The expiration date of the policyholder's passport or custom identifier.

date_of_birth.description: (Individual policyholders only) The policyholder's date of birth in the format YYYYMMDD. Forbidden if `id.type` is "id" and `id.country` is "ZA".

gender.description: (Individual policyholders only) The gender of the policyholder.

first_name.description: Policyholder or primary contact's legal first name.

middle_name.description: The policyholder or primary contact's legal middle name.

last_name.description: Policyholder or primary contact's legal last name.

email.description: `null` is allowed. Policyholder or primary contact's contact email address.

app_data.description: `null` is allowed. An object containing additional custom data for the policyholder.

company_name.description: The name of the company that the policyholder represents. Only required when `type = company`.

registration_number.description: The company's registration number. Only required when `type = company`.

date_of_establishment.description: (Company policyholders only) The company's date of establishment in the format YYYYMMDD. Required when `type = company`.

company_website_url.description: (Company policyholders only) The company's website URL. Required when `type = company`.

subsidiary_companies.description: (Company policyholders only) An array of subsidiary company names. Required when `type = company`.

contact_position.description: (Company policyholders only) The position or role of the primary contact at the company. Required when `type = company`.

address.description: `null` is allowed. The policyholder's physical address. See below for more details.

line_1.description: First line of the policyholder's address.

line_2.description: `null` is allowed. Optional second line of the policyholder's address.

country.description: The policyholder's country code, in the [ISO Alpha-2](http://www.nationsonline.org/oneworld/country_code_list.htm) format.

area_code.description: The policyholder's area code, must be 4 characters long when the policyholder's country code is ZA.

payment-details.description: Details of the account into which the payout should be made.

type.description: The type of account into which the payout should be made.  For a beneficiary, this must be `eft`.

branch_code.description: The branch code for the account.

beneficiary-relationship.description: The beneficiary's relationship to the policyholder.

payment_method_id.description: Must be a UUID. The Unique identifier for the payment method.

type.description: The payment method type. Except for external payment methods, must be one of `debit_order`, `card`, `eft`, `collection_module`. For external payment methods, will correspond to the custom `key` field.

created_at.description: The date and time at which the payment method was created.

updated_at.description: The date and time at which the payment method was last updated.

organization_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the related organisation.

policyholder_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the policyholder the payment method is linked to.

policyholder.description: The policyholder object linked to the payment method. Only included when `?include=policyholder` is specified.

policies.description: An array of policy objects linked to the payment method. Only included when `?include=policies` is specified.

bank_details.description: Required if `type` is `debit_order`. Bank details for the debit order.

account_holder_identification.description: `null` is allowed. An object containing the identification details of the account holder.

type.description: The type of identity document.

country.description: The [ISO Alpha-2](http://www.nationsonline.org/oneworld/country_code_list.htm) country code of the country of the identification document.

expiration_date.description: The expiration date of the lead's passport.

blocked_reason.description: The reason the payment method is blocked. Only relevant if `banv_status` is `blocked`.

banv_response.description: The response received when bank account verification was attempted.

payment_method_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the payment method.

card.description: Details of the payment card. Required if `type` is `card`.

expiry_month.description: The month of the card's exiry date.

expiry_year.description: The year of the card's exiry date.

last_4_digits.description: The last four digits of the card number.

registration.description: The registration ID of the card.

external_reference.description: The external identifier of the payment method. Required if `type` is `external`.

banv_status.description: The [bank account verification status](doc:account-verification-banvavs#reporting-on-payment-methods). Only relevant if `type` is `external`.

blocked_reason.description: The reason the payment method is blocked. Only relevant if `banv_status` is `blocked` and `type` is `external`.

collection_module_definition_id.description: The unique identifier of the collection module definition that the payment method is linked to. Only relevant if `type` is `collection_module`.

collection_module_key.description: The key of the collection module that the payment method is linked to. Only relevant if `type` is `collection_module`.

module.description: Custom module-specific data for collection module payment methods. Only relevant if `type` is `collection_module`.

collection_module.description: The collection module object linked to the payment method. Only included when `?include=collection_module` is specified and `type` is `collection_module`.

updated_by.description: An object indicating the user or API key that last updated the payment method. Only relevant if `type` is `collection_module`.

secret_key_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the secret key.

environment.description: The environment associated with the secret key.

description.description: A description of the secret key.

type.description: This field uniquely identifies the secret key.

key.description: The encrypted value of the secret key.

created_at.description: Time at which the secret key was created.

created_by.description: An object indicating the user or API key that created the secret key.

type.description: The type of the creator (e.g., user, API).

updated_at.description: Time at which the secret key was last updated.

updated_by.description: An object indicating the user or API key that last updated the secret key.

type.description: The type of the updater (e.g., user, API).

fulfillment_request_id.name: fulfillment_request_id
fulfillment_request_id.description: Must be a UUID. The unique identifier of the fulfillment request.

data_store_key.name: data_store_key
data_store_key.description: The unique key of the [data store](ref:data-store)

page_size.name: page_size
page_size.description: The maximum number of items that will be contained in a single page, between 1 and 100 (inclusive).

page.name: page
page.description: The page number of the entities to fetch. Must be a minimum of 1.

idempotency.name: idempotency_key
idempotency.description: To perform an idempotent request, provide the `idempotency_key` query parameter with the request. An idempotency key is a unique value generated by the client which the platform uses to recognize subsequent retries of the same request. How you create unique keys is up to you, but we suggest using a V4 UUID, or another random string with enough entropy to avoid collisions. Idempotency keys can be up to 255 characters long.

webhook_queue_event_subscriptions.name: subscriptions
webhook_queue_event_subscriptions.description: Filter queue events by subscription (comma-separated list).

webhook_queue_event_from.name: from
webhook_queue_event_from.description: Filter for queue events created on or after this time (ISO 8601 format).

webhook_queue_event_to.name: to
webhook_queue_event_to.description: Filter for queue events created on or before this time (ISO 8601 format).
